title: 50 Kroner "Karusse" (Painting and Animal)

obverse.description:
Blue print on white paper.
At the right a portrait of Mrs. Engelke Charlotte Ryberg after a painting from 1797 by Jens Juel.
obverse.lettering:
Number
DANMARKS 
NATIONALBANK
50
FEMTI KRONER serial#
DANMARKS
NATIONALBANK
2 signatures
obverse.lettering_translation:
Nationalbank of Denmark
Fifty Kroner
Nationalbank of Denmark

reverse.description:
Blue print on white paper.
Crucian carp after drawing by Ib Andersen
reverse.lettering:
50 DANMARKS NATIONALBANK 50
50
FEMTI KRONER
Serie 1972
Udstedt i henhold til
Lov af 7. April 1936
reverse.lettering_translation:
Nationalbank of Denmark
Fifty Kroner
Serie 1972
Issued according to Law of 7th April 1936

comments:
Withdrawel:
Withdrawn since: 7th May 1999
 

Year:

The two middle digits of the blue series number give the last two digits of the year of issue; e.g. D4971D =(19)97.

 

Variants in security thread:

Series A0 to A6 have a light security thread (DNF# 8.3.1)
Series C0 through D5 have a dark security thread (DNF# 8.3.2)

 

Nickname:

Due to the animals on this series of notes, they are sometimes affectionately called by their featured creatures. This is especially the case with the higher denominations. Hence this note can be called a "karusse" in Danish.
 

Signatures:
The left signature is one of the 3 Governors of DANMARKS NATIONALBANK.
The right signature on this note is always the “Chefen for Seddelfaget” (Head of Notes). These were Valeur (1972-1979), Billestrup (1982-1985), Herly (1989-1995) and Heering (1996-1998).

watermark.description: Self-portrait of Jens Juel and "50" repeated vertically.
